ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Xegi banama gi bɛdimɛ ni mlɔ ko mlɔ kyɛ mlɛdimɛ nɔ ɛ, mlakígyo le edi si Aya aatsa liʋɛ tɔtɔ ɔki mlɔ ɔ. Lɛ ese linu si ligbãzeze lɛ klɔ elu lilɛ babɔgyɔ ba fɛ mɛ ɛ.
translated: The old woman and the children went to the farm, but they didn't meet the farmer there, because he had gone to Aya's place to buy a chicken. The woman and the children were left alone at home, and they were very hungry.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Lɛ loso e, mlakíse lifu u. Lɛ ese linu si, mlɛŋa kedzi isani abia likpi. Yesu yɛ su kudradra ni yɛ kugbegbe
translated: The book is on the table. The dog is very big, it is as big as a donkey. Jesus is good to us and loves us

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Agi Yesu asɛ niklɔ ɛ, atrɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ.
translated: Jesus Christ was born, he was betrayed by Judas to be killed.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Lɛ banama gi beze ní yɛ su i, ba mɛ tole asi Yesu si, “Dike, wone ni wɔ beneminyime ba balɛ ní oŋwe no, gi bɛɛpɛ si beegu kunugu ni wo o.”
translated: The people who were there at the time said to Jesus, "Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery, right in the act."

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Lɛ ese linu si asi yɛ isu ya si, “Xe màtani yɛ liwu le ɔgyurɔ lɔ tètè kugbɔnɔ ko e, maayɔ.”
translated: The one who is going to the river says, "When I arrive at the river, I will throw a stone into it, and it will splash."

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: Pɔ̀ gi Okusi ye etiku tɔ yidi bababa ba ɛ, amɔ onyime ɔtɔ gi ɔ́kpɛ kudzɛgbanɔɔ liwu le e.
translated: The chief of Okusi and his elders were sitting under a tree, and the young man was sitting on a separate stool beside them.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtsa limu ne lɛ beple eku ní Genesaret kesea mɛ; lɛ bɛka katapokpaa ní limu ne kutu yɔ ɛ.
translated: Jesus got into a boat and sat down, while the crowd was on the land at the shore of the lake of Gennesaret.

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
text: Lɛ asi ba si, “Bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ni Aya yɛ liboe le me sì Aya apani sì, ‘Mɛ Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, biaxwa lɛ si, ligba gi biazesumu me ní lɛ mɛ ɛ. ‘Pɔ̀ mlɛ kɔ lɛ ɛbitɛ okpokɔ ki beze ba!”
translated: They said, "Tell Aya that the one who is coming to Aya's home says, 'I am Lisɔligba, the child is mine, the child's mother is in my hands.' 'Don't let him come and take the child away from me!"

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Gi Yesu asɛ ní klɔ ɛ, emu ketokpa katɔ lɛ axwa banama gi apɛ ɛ, lɛ bɛba yɛ klɔ ɛ.
translated: Jesus said to them, the one who wants to be first among you, will be the last of all and your servant.

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Kitɛ mɛ abla bebi ba bɛnyani xe béte Aya kyɛ na a! Mleʋi si mibitɛ linugutseboe! Owo lítra ba lɛ mɛ ɛ! Linugutseboe tile gi biaki mlɔ ɛ, lɛ ko linu Yona oboedoplɛɛ su linugutseboe le e.
translated: Jesus said to them, "A wicked and adulterous generation asks for a sign! But none will be given it except the sign of the prophet Jonah."

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Lɛ Yesu axwa ba manɔ ɔ, xe egu kunugu yo aki ba ní abee ʋa me si, “Kitɛ Abosam yɛ aatani abosam yɛ kukpikpinɔ dono e?
translated: And Jesus said to them, "What did the evil spirit say when it came out from the man?

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Lɛ Zebedeo yedze aba Yesu klɔ ni yɛ bebinyime tiaʋa ba ɛ. Edu yɛ aklamasi na kesea akí yɛ, lɛ eʋi si ɔpɛ lipo me ɔki yɛ ɛ.
translated: Then Zebedee's sons came to Jesus and asked him, "Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask."

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Aga atrɛ ɔtɔ nɔ taʋɛ ko lɛ amɔ beneminyime tiaʋa dzedze e, Yakobo nì Yohanes. Beze Zebedeo yɛ bebi. Beze ba katapokpaa mɛ gi bɛzɛɛlulɔ ba dzikle ʋa a.
translated: Going on from there, he saw two other brothers, James son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with their father Zebedee, preparing their nets.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Lɛ kidɔ kakaa gi bedzi idɔnɔ kedzia mɛ ɛ, linu si beplo ke ni tɔ beplo ba awla la ní ɔtɔpu kɔ xe ableke biaŋa bidɔ ɔ. Isã dzedze itɔ gi ba bakatsi ba bɛkpɛ ɛplɛ, ŋwisi kupliʋi yo, kudɔtɔfukpoo, ni kudɔkpaa liɔ mɛ bizɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ, bɛ kiploploe ní ba egoʋi la onu petee, bɛzɛbitɛ ní lɛ aʋa.
translated: The child who does not listen to the words of the elderly will one day suffer, because when the elderly people in the village are discussing something and they ask him or her to go and bring something, he or she will not be able to, and they will say that he or she is a useless child, and he or she will be ashamed, and they will not give him or her any food, and he or she will be hungry.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Lɛ Ozimizi ye aba yɛ klɔ ɔ; lɛ asi yɛ si, “Xe Aya Yebi wonu i, kɔlɔ ki tɔ akla la ya edzì bidɔŋaŋa aki wɔ.”
translated: The elders say that long ago; they said, "If Aya Yebi sees you, you will be given a lot of food and you will eat."

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Niteme liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ Yeremya apani aplɛ ɛ,
translated: The book of the prophet Jeremiah is in the Bible.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Mledi liwɔ lɛ mɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ; kuʋíkpɛ bidɔ loo vini bidɔ kɔbla ní owle me, pɔ fɛ ɛ mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne e izeedi kɔ aʋa. Mláŋa kedzi asani kɔ na a?
translated: The child is crying and I don't know what to do; I've given it food and water but it refuses, and I don't know what's wrong with it, perhaps it's the work of the gods that's affecting it. What medicine can I use to heal it?

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: Lɛ ese linu si wɔ tutɔ wɔ eboe la biakɔ tsã litsi le ɔki wɔ, lɛ wɔ tutɔ wɔ eboe la biakɔ bu wɔ lipɔ.” Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ ni Farisianɛatɔ beʋi i dɔni Yesu awla si ɔbitɛ linugutseboe
translated: The one who is in heaven is the one who will help us, the one who is on earth is the one who will trouble us.” He said this to the Pharisees who were with Jesus and were trying to test him

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: “Mleze ní aŋuplɛplɛ mɛ ní asia na beboedoplɛ ba su, lɛ ese linu si biaba mlɔ klɔ ŋwisi iʋesi kinɛ ní aŋu mɛ. Pɔ̀ ní kimɛ mɛ ɛ, banyraga ɔtutɔ benu u.
translated: “The stranger and the traveler are the same, and the foreigner and the beggar are one, because they all rely on the charity of others to survive. So if you see me, don’t look down on me.”

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
text: Gi kuʋɛɔ kwize kume e, mlɛki mɛ kiŋaŋa, olile ezeku me lɛ mlɛki mɛ kuni; Meze ɔbaba lɛ mlɛzi mɛ kpɛ mlɔ kupeo me.
translated: The farmer went to the farm, the wife stayed at home, she cooked food for the wife of the farmer; The woman's husband stayed with the wife to peel the cassava for her.

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Kese kiayɔ kedu ní kese su, lɛ kukusi kwiata kigũ ni kukusi, kuʋɛ kwiadɔ lɛ kese kiahwa ni ikɔ glegle e.
translated: The big house and the small house, the big farm and the small farm, the big road and the small road are all the same to death.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.

translated: The women went to the tomb, but they found the stone rolled away from it. They went inside the tomb and did not find the body of Jesus there. They went inside and found it empty, and they saw a young man sitting on the right side.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.

translated: The farmer went to the farm and saw that the corn was ripe, so he called the children to come and harvest it.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?

translated: If Aya's yam is not good and it's not tasty, and the goat didn't eat the leaves, what would have made the yam become so bitter? The yam is very bitter!

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!

translated: The bad person's child is also a bad person! Can the tiny bird that is in the sky come down and catch the cat and the mouse for me?

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.

translated: Jesus Christ said to his disciples and said "I am the way" "Disciples said you are the way we should follow to have eternal life with God.

ref: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
text: Banɔ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba klɔ gi akya ɛ, ba benu: Simon .
translated: They went to the farm and when they got there, they saw him, they said: Simon.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Banɔatɔ bɛgbani banuvɔ ba ɛ mani Yesu klɔ tɔ ɔtrɔ awla ní ba aʋa ni tɔ ɔxwa Aya ní ba litukpo, pɔ̀ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtɛni banɔaklɔ ɔ.
translated: The disciples went to the village where Jesus was going to pray with them and they stood on the mountain and he prayed to God and they slept, but he stood praying to his Father.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Nite si mlaate ba gani ba kiboebitɛɛ mɛ ɛ. Litsixwawe le su bidɔ basɛbasɛ
translated: The person who has not traveled thinks his mother is the best cook. Traveling around the world broadens one's knowledge

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Lɛ Petro ebu le edo si, “Dike, ɔka! kwiyurɔ kidɔ kakaa, lɛ kwikuni wɔ kude e. Ege kwiamɔ obu ní lɛ mɛ ɛ?”
translated: Then Petro said to him, "Look, friend! the squirrel's tail is long, but it does not sweep the ground. Does your wife's hair reach the ground?"

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Agi Yesu edziitrɛ Yerusalem keletitia livlɛ lɛ ɛ, kuʋe kwizeeku ye.
translated: Jesus went to Jerusalem to the big festival there, and many people saw him.

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Pɔ̀ mɛɛsi mlɔ abla si mlakíʋi kuwle aki ɔnɔnɔ ɔ. Xe ɔnutɔ ábɔ wɔ bidɔ ní ɔŋawla lɔ kutu yɔ mɛ ɛ, ki tɔ ɔbɔ wɔ ní kaʋaa kuni yɔ mɛ kyɛ.
translated: The woman and the man are working on the farm. The woman is weeding and the man is making mounds for the yams.

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Lɛ Yesu anina yɛ ɔwla lɔ xe agbɔni onyimee e, lɛ asi yɛ si, “Èe, linu mɛ kupɛpɛ. Wɔ isu ya ìdra a!”
translated: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he withdrew to Galilee, and said, "The time has come. The kingdom of God is near!"

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Lɛ kposɔ sipi liwu Yohanes akpɛ, lɛ akɔ kagaplekpa ɔgɛmi akɔ anya yɛ kasɛa mɛ ɛ. Yɛ kiŋaŋaɛ kize akikli ni liŋwafu mɛ kìbu.
translated: When John's father died, he went to Kagaplekpa and he was given a small piece of land to farm. The land was full of weeds and small trees and shrubs.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Lifu liku banɔpe ba nyani nyani sì besugu nite si bɛfɛ ŋwisi banɔ gi betse na a.
translated: The tortoise and the monkey went to the forest to look for food to eat but they didn't find any.

ref: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
text: kɔ ekpese yɛ bahanuglɔ banɔsumu ba kukusi, lɛ aaŋa kɔ aŋwɛ ni badaŋwɛmu ba ɛ.
translated: The tortoise and the hare went to the forest to dig yams, but the tortoise's yams were more than the hare's.

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Agi Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aba ɛ, azaaŋa bidɔ, lɛ azaaŋwɛ wain yɛ kyɛ, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa apani si, ‘Mledi onyime liɛ ya a! onu ɔŋazi nɛ ni ɔdaŋwɛmu, babɔgyɔ ba ni “banɔ zimizi ba” ɔʋia nɛ.’ Kuboeteo kɔ kiboebitɛ kɛ kizebu isu ya ɛbasɛ si kwina a.” Imaɛ lɛ klɔ gi izi Yesu yɛ liboe le aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ
translated: The chief of Yebi said, "When a child is born, we take a fowl, we give the child water to drink, and we call the child's name, saying, 'May Mledi agree that you be strong! May you be a great hunter, and may your people be numerous.' We do this so that the child will be strong and healthy. And we know that Jesus is the one who gives strength.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “ ‘Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ’ lɛ ese linu si mlɔ pɔ́ ɛ, benemi ko mlenu lɛ ɔdɔbasɛ tole ko ɔlɛ ní mlɔ awla.
translated: “ ‘The hunter’s wife’ they say is not allowed to eat oil, but the hunter himself can eat oil with his wife.”

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Lɛ loso magbani yɛ amani wɔ badɔkpasi ba, pɔ̀ bátani yɛ kuyɔnɔ nɔ ɔ.”
translated: The stranger's fowl lays eggs in the landlord's farm, but the landlord's fowl lays eggs in the stranger's farm.
